items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed — here's why

children.content: The youngest of the Snowtown killers was the subject of a long-running suppression order that kept his face — or any description of his appearance — hidden for 25 years. Here's why that's been lifted.

articles.title: Snowtown killer's suppression order lifts after 25 years

children.content: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image can be revealed after South Australia's highest court lifted a suppression order made 25 years ago.

image.altText: A court sketch of Snowtown killer James Vlassakis.

articles.title: SA Parole Board says commissioner made 'error' in overturning Snowtown killer's release

children.content: New documents accuse the Parole Administrative Review Commissioner of making a mistake by not allowing the SA Parole Board to participate in a review of a decision to release Snowtown serial killer James Vlassakis.

image.altText: The old bank at the township of Snowtown.

gallery.altText: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis in custody.

thumbnail.altText: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis in custody.

items.title: X fights Andrew and Tristan Tate's bid to unmask anonymous critics

children.content: Misogynist influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have struck a hurdle in their bid to unmask anonymous users they allege have defamed them, with social media platform X asking a Florida court to dismiss the motion.

image.altText: two men standing near each other

items.title: Paedophile former magistrate to be released on home detention

children.content: Paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy will be released from jail when his sentence expires next month, despite an ongoing application to have him indefinitely detained.

image.altText: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

items.title: New move to change SA's abortion laws goes before state parliament

children.content: South Australia's parliament is set to debate restricting abortion access for the third time in less than two years, with One Nation's breakthrough election result meaning the laws could pass the upper house.

items.title: Man killed mother with tool while under 'supernatural' attack, court hears

children.content: An Adelaide Hills man standing trial for the murder of his mother describes to the Supreme Court how he repeatedly struck her while he was panicked and "under attack" by "supernatural beings".
Warning: This story contains images and details that may be distressing to some readers.

items.title: Man charged with rape after alleged Adelaide Hills home invasion

children.content: A man who allegedly broke into a home and sexually assaulted the occupant will face court charged with aggravated serious criminal trespass and rape.

items.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

items.title: Landscape in outback SA 'permanently changed' by recent earthquake

items.title: 'Survival mode all the time': Australian parents break taboos on raising kids

items.title: Inquest hears elderly man who died was given another person's medication

children.content: SA Power Networks is investing about $10 million to combat insulator pollution, which has been linked to repeated power outages across regional South Australia over summer.

items.title: They might look the same, but these penguins are very different

children.content: Gentoo penguins were thought to be a standalone species, but a new study says there are four distinct species — and it could lead to better conservation plans.

items.title: Deciding to stay and rebuild after 'one-in-500-year' flood

children.content: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mis North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.

items.title: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across the outback

children.content: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

items.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?

children.content: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.

image.altText: Green crops in the foreground with machinery in the background.

items.title: Why the future of one of Queensland's last true fishing towns is on the brink

children.content: Karumba's rich fishing industry dates back to the roaring, prawning era of the 60s, but major blows are seeing a seafood decline. However, the Gulf of Carpentaria residents say they "wouldn't live anywhere else".
